disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intense generalised itch that is characteristically far worse at night and has escalated over the last 5 days on a background of a 3-week insidious onset
trigger_question_examples: When did the itch start and how has it changed?
Is the itch worse at any particular time of day?
Talk me through how the last few weeks have gone.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started about three weeks ago with a few itchy bumps, but the last five days it's been non-stop and it's far and away worst at night once I'm warm in bed — that's what's stopping me sleeping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A close household contact (the spouse) has developed the same itch and rash within days, sharing a bed and living spaces
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone else in the house started itching?
Is anyone you live with or share a bed with affected?
Has anyone in close contact developed anything similar?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — my wife has started getting the exact same itchy bumps and night-time itch over the last day or two. We share a bed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Distribution favours the finger-webs, flexor wrists, periumbilical skin, axillae and the genitalia, with thread-like lines in the finger-web spaces and itchy nodules in the groin
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the itch and the rash?
Is it in any particular spots — hands, tummy, groin?
Can you describe where the bumps are?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on my wrists, around my belly button, in my armpits and my thighs, and itchy down in the groin. Between my fingers there are a few tiny thread-like greyish lines.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is afebrile and systemically well — no fever, no feeling generally unwell
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever or feel hot and shivery?
Are you otherwise well in yourself apart from the itch?
Any signs you're generally unwell, not just the skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever — I took my temperature at home and it was normal, and I don't feel hot or unwell. It's really just the itch and the exhaustion from no sleep.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really thought about a fever, I've been so focused on the itch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No thick honey-coloured crusting, no localised hot fluctuant swelling, and no spreading warm redness in the excoriated areas
trigger_question_examples: Are any of the scratched areas oozing thick yellow crust or pus?
Is any part hot, swollen or spreading red?
Do any of the raw spots look infected — weeping yellow crust, a hot lump?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No thick yellow crust or pus, and nothing feels hot or swollen — the raw bits are just where I've scratched them open, but there's no spreading redness that I can see.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: They just look scratched and sore to me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Over-the-counter antihistamines have provided no relief
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for it so far?
Have any treatments helped at all?
What have you used and did it work?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I bought antihistamine tablets from the chemist but they did absolutely nothing for the itch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe sleep deprivation over 72 hours with daytime fatigue, reduced concentration and psychological distress
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting your sleep and day-to-day life?
How are you coping with it?
Is it affecting your work or concentration?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've barely slept in three days because the itch is unbearable at night. I'm exhausted, I can't concentrate at work, and it's really getting me down.
